description: Cross-repo contribution heatmap CLI command showing GitHub-style green squares locally. Aggregates commit counts per calendar day across all tracked repos. Rendered in-terminal via ratatui with color intensity tiers.

userStories.title: DB query: daily commit counts over arbitrary date range
userStories.description: Add `query_daily_commit_counts(conn, from, to) -> BTreeMap<NaiveDate, u32>` in query.rs. Groups git_activity rows where event_type='commit' by local calendar date. Returns zero-padded map (every date in range has an entry).
acceptanceCriteria: Function signature: `pub fn query_daily_commit_counts(conn: &Connection, from: DateTime<Utc>, to: DateTime<Utc>) -> anyhow::Result<BTreeMap<NaiveDate, u32>>`
SQL: SELECT date(timestamp, 'localtime') as day, COUNT(*) FROM git_activity WHERE event_type='commit' AND timestamp >= ?1 AND timestamp <= ?2 GROUP BY day
All dates in [from.date(), to.date()] present in result (zero-filled gaps)
Returns empty map (not error) when no commits exist in range
Unit test: insert 3 commits across 2 days, verify counts and zero-fill for gap day

userStories.title: HeatmapData struct and intensity calculation
userStories.description: Define `HeatmapData` in a new `src/heatmap.rs` module. Holds the daily counts and computes color intensity tier (0-4) matching GitHub's scale: 0=none, 1=low, 2=medium, 3=high, 4=max.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `src/heatmap.rs` declared in `src/lib.rs`
`pub struct HeatmapData { pub days: BTreeMap<NaiveDate, u32>, pub max_count: u32 }`
`impl HeatmapData { pub fn from_counts(counts: BTreeMap<NaiveDate, u32>) -> Self }` — computes max_count
`pub fn intensity(&self, date: NaiveDate) -> u8` returns 0-4: 0=zero commits, 1=1..=max/4, 2..=3 proportional, 4=>=3*max/4 (or >= some floor like 4 commits)
Edge case: max_count==0 → all intensity 0
Unit tests: zero map → all 0; single commit → intensity >= 1; high count → intensity 4

userStories.title: Date range helper: last N weeks aligned to calendar weeks
userStories.description: Add `heatmap_range(weeks: u32) -> (DateTime<Utc>, DateTime<Utc>)` in query.rs. Returns range from `weeks` Mondays ago (00:00 local) through end of today. Default 52 weeks (1 year).
acceptanceCriteria: Function: `pub fn heatmap_range(weeks: u32) -> (DateTime<Utc>, DateTime<Utc>)`
Start = Monday of (current_week - weeks) at 00:00 local
End = today 23:59:59 local (or now)
weeks=1 → returns 7-14 days depending on current weekday
weeks=52 → returns ~364 days
Unit test: range is always <= weeks*7+6 days and starts on a Monday

userStories.title: Terminal renderer: ratatui heatmap widget
userStories.description: Add `render_heatmap(frame, area, data: &HeatmapData, weeks: u32)` in `src/heatmap.rs`. Renders GitHub-style grid: columns=weeks (left=oldest), rows=7 (Mon top). Each cell = 1 char block colored by intensity tier.
acceptanceCriteria: Color map: 0=DarkGray, 1=Color::Rgb(0,68,0), 2=Color::Rgb(0,128,0), 3=Color::Rgb(0,185,0), 4=Color::Rgb(57,211,83) — 5 distinct tiers
Cell char: full-block `██` (two chars wide for square feel) or single `█`
Month labels rendered above first column of each month
Day-of-week labels on left (M W F or Mon/Wed/Fri abbreviated)
Renders without panic on terminal width < grid width (truncate weeks to fit)
Test: `render_heatmap` with TestBackend(120,10) and empty HeatmapData does not panic

userStories.title: CLI command: `blackbox heatmap`
userStories.description: Wire up `blackbox heatmap` command. Renders full-year (52-week) heatmap to terminal using ratatui in raw mode, then exits. Shows summary line: total commits, active days, longest streak.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `Heatmap { #[arg(long, default_value_t = 52)] weeks: u32 }` variant to `Commands` enum in cli.rs
Match arm in main.rs calls `blackbox::heatmap::run_heatmap(weeks)`
Public `pub fn run_heatmap(weeks: u32) -> anyhow::Result<()>` in heatmap.rs
Loads config + DB same pattern as run_query in main.rs
Renders grid + summary line (total commits, active days, longest streak)
Exits cleanly (no interactive loop — static render then return)
`blackbox heatmap --help` shows description

userStories.title: Summary stats: active days and longest streak
userStories.description: Add `HeatmapStats` struct computed from `HeatmapData`. Fields: `total_commits: u32`, `active_days: u32`, `longest_streak: u32`, `current_streak: u32`.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub struct HeatmapStats { pub total_commits: u32, pub active_days: u32, pub longest_streak: u32, pub current_streak: u32 }`
`impl HeatmapData { pub fn stats(&self) -> HeatmapStats }`
longest_streak: max consecutive calendar days with commits >= 1
current_streak: streak ending on today (or yesterday if today==0)
Unit tests: 0-day streak on empty data; streak=3 for 3 consecutive days; streak resets on gap

userStories.title: Static render mode (non-interactive, stdout-compatible)
userStories.description: `run_heatmap` should render to stdout as ANSI-colored text (not full ratatui raw mode) so it works in pipes/scripts. Use colored crate for intensity tiers instead of ratatui. Ratatui render remains available as a separate function for future TUI integration.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub fn render_heatmap_ansi(data: &HeatmapData, weeks: u32) -> String` in heatmap.rs using colored crate
`run_heatmap` calls `render_heatmap_ansi` and prints to stdout — no ratatui raw mode
Output includes grid + month labels + day labels + stats line
Works correctly when piped (colors may be absent but no crash)
ratatui-based `render_heatmap` widget fn still exists for future TUI embedding
Integration test: `blackbox heatmap` exits with code 0 via assert_cmd

userStories.title: Custom week range flag
userStories.description: Support `--weeks N` to show last N weeks (default 52). Validate 1 <= N <= 260 (5 years).
acceptanceCriteria: `blackbox heatmap --weeks 12` renders 12-week grid
`blackbox heatmap --weeks 0` returns error: 'weeks must be between 1 and 260'
`blackbox heatmap --weeks 261` returns error same
Validation in `run_heatmap` before DB query
Unit test: heatmap_range(12) returns ~84 day span

userStories.title: Integration test: heatmap command end-to-end
userStories.description: Add `tests/heatmap.rs`. Insert known commits into temp DB via direct SQL, run `blackbox heatmap`, assert exit 0 and output contains block chars.
acceptanceCriteria: Test inserts 5 commits across 3 different dates in temp DB
`blackbox heatmap` exits with code 0
stdout contains at least one colored block char (\u2588 or equivalent indicator)
Test uses assert_cmd + tempfile pattern (same as existing integration tests)
No daemon required for test

userStories.title: Empty state handling
userStories.description: Graceful output when no commits exist in range.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty DB: prints grid of all dark-gray cells + message 'No commits recorded in this period'
Stats line shows 0/0/0
Does not panic or error
Test: run `blackbox heatmap` against empty DB, assert exit 0 and output contains 'No commits'

userStories.title: Update README.md and AGENTS.md
userStories.description: Update project documentation to reflect new feature. README.md: add new command to Commands table if applicable, update feature descriptions. AGENTS.md: add new source files to Architecture tree, update Key Patterns if new patterns introduced.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md Commands table includes new command (if a CLI command was added)
README.md describes the new feature/capability
AGENTS.md Architecture src/ tree lists any new .rs files
AGENTS.md Key Dependencies updated if new crates added
userStories.notes: This must be the LAST story executed. Reference README.md and AGENTS.md in project root.
